init_file: Run SQL file on startup
Ceph: Build your own cluster on CentOS
Ceph is an object, a block and filesystem storage system.
As you can see from the Ceph.com visitors map
, people all around the world search for it. You can also see some metrics about the Ceph project at their metrics site

I'm going to go over the installation of a two node Ceph storage cluster.
You can then build on those storage nodes and choose multiple storage types you want to use.
I personally recommend to use at least two nodes even for a test cluster!
I assume that you have basic linux knowledge and at least two servers with CentOS7 up and running that can reach each other.
In my case my two example servers are having 2vCores and 4GB RAM. The OS used is CentOS7 (to be exact

).
My examples servers for this tutorial have the following addresses:
the IPs used here are according to
You have to decide from what server you want to deploy your cluster.

Let's begin first by adding the Epel and Ceph repository to all our used servers.
On all the servers we have, we need to add the Epel package repositories to the repository list.
The following commands add the Epel package repositories and the Epel repository verification key:
language-bash shiki shiki-themes material-theme-lighter material-theme material-theme-palenight

sudo yum install -y yum-utils && sudo yum-config-manager --add-repo https://dl.fedoraproject.org/pub/epel/7/x86_64/
sudo yum install --nogpgcheck -y epel-release
sudo rpm --import /etc/pki/rpm-gpg/RPM-GPG-KEY-EPEL-7
sudo rm /etc/yum.repos.d/dl.fedoraproject.org*

Now that our servers have the Epel repository added, we're finally going to add the Ceph repository.
Use your favorited editor to create a file
and copy the following content into it:
language-ini shiki shiki-themes material-theme-lighter material-theme material-theme-palenight

[ceph-noarch]
name=Ceph noarch packages
baseurl=http://download.ceph.com/rpm-jewel/el7/noarch
enabled=1
gpgcheck=1
type=rpm-md
gpgkey=https://download.ceph.com/keys/release.asc
with the Ceph release version you want to install (current latest release is
with, depending on what CentOS version number you have, for example for CentOS7 it should be
and for CentOS6 it should be
(other CentOS versions don't have official rpm builds available and will not work!).

Now we have the Ceph repository in our repository list on all of our servers.
An important step is to configure NTP service on all servers to keep the time synchronized across the storage cluster, but I'm not going to cover this.
Other important things to make sure, before continuing with the tutorial:
You should use an user with password less login and sudo without password rights on details how to create an user for that see
Selinux needs to be set to
during the installation process (Command to disable SElinux temporarly
By default firewalld is in place on most CentOS servers, you either have to open the specific ports or just disable the firewall completely (for more details see
Switch to your deployment server and install the Ceph deployment package
on the deployment server, in my case server

.
The first commands will update your package database and system. The second command will install the

sudo yum clean all && sudo yum update
sudo yum install ceph-deploy
Still on the deployment server (in my case

, we see the generated files in the directory:
Now we need to add an option to the bottom of the default Ceph configuration file
The option sets the number of replications, for two nodes you

else the Ceph cluster will not become healthy.
You can also add other options here, but as this is just a basic tutorial on how to create a Ceph cluster I'm not covering other options than the default.

is ready we are going to install Ceph on all servers and create our initial cluster monitor.
The command for deploying Ceph onto my two servers and creating the monitor server on your server chosen above, in my case

ceph-deploy install --no-adjust-repos ceph-tutorial-node1 ceph-tutorial-node2
ceph-deploy mon create-initial
If the command fails, you need to apply a bug fix with the command

If the last command exits with errors, check the connectivity of your servers and firewall between the servers (if a firewall is in place between the servers, make sure it allows the Ceph ports, ports: TCP Ceph mon(itor) 6789, Ceph OSDs 6800:7300 (for more information see
Use ssh to connect to your servers that you want to be storage nodes and create a directory for the storaged data.

ssh ceph-tutorial-node1
sudo mkdir /var/local/osd
exit

ssh ceph-tutorial-node2
sudo mkdir /var/local/osd
exit
Back on your deployment server (in my case
) we are going to prepare the storage nodes with the following command:
For my two servers I run:

prepares the storage location and sets up the Ceph OSD in the background.
The only thing we have to do now is to activate the storage locations (Ceph OSDs) using the activate command:
Copy the Ceph admin key to the servers you want to be able to configure your Ceph cluster from with the below command:
is your server you ran all the commands on.
To check if you Ceph cluster is healthy use the health command:
If it shows something else than
your cluster is unhealthy and may not even work properly.
That's all. Now you have a working Ceph storage cluster.
In case of other problems, all Ceph log files are located under

# ls -hl /var/log/ceph/
total 340K
-rw------- 1 root root 126K Aug  8 13:24 ceph.log
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 196K Aug  8 13:25 ceph-mon.ceph-tutorial-node1.log
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 5.2K Aug  8 09:26 ceph-osd.0.log
If the health command returns a message like
, then your clock on the server(s) is not synced. Please install and configure an NTP server and client(s).

This should help you get started with installing/running a Ceph cluster on CentOS.
If you have issues, please refer to the official
ceph-deploy - Deploy Ceph with minimal infrastructure - ceph-deploy 2.0.0 documentation
